The Wolf of Wall Street (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $116,949,183Details
International Box Office $272,866,953
Worldwide Box Office $389,816,136
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $20,156,026
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $20,022,614
Total $40,178,640
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Martin Scorsese directs the story of New York stockbroker Jordan Belfort. From the American dream to corporate greed, Belfort goes from penny stocks and righteousness to IPOs and a life of corruption in the late 80s. Excess success and affluence in his early twenties as founder of the brokerage firm Stratton Oakmont warranted Belfort the title "The Wolf of Wall Street."

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$18,410,067 (15.7% of total gross)
Legs:6.35 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:30.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$100,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,537 opening theaters/2,557 max. theaters, 6.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $171,499,882

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2013 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: December 25th, 2013 (Wide) (France)
December 25th, 2013 (Wide) (Switzerland)
December 26th, 2013 (Wide) (Croatia)
December 26th, 2013 (Wide) (Greece)
December 26th, 2013 (Wide) (Hungary)
Video Release: March 25th, 2014 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: R for sequences of strong sexual content, graphic nudity, drug use and language throughout, and for some violence.
(Rating bulletin 2300, 11/27/2013)
Running Time: 165 minutes
Award nomination: 2014 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: Breaking the Fourth Wall, Digital Cinematography, Intertitle, Prologue, Voiceover/Narration
Filming location (country): United States
Plot point: Addiction, FBI, Infidelity, Mafia, Narcotics, Non-Chronological, Prescription Drug Abuse, Prostitution, White Collar Crime
Social setting: Wall Street
Subgenre: Black Comedy

Genre:Black Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Appian Way, Sikelia Productions, Emjag
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Martin Scorsese directs the story of New York stockbroker Jordan Belfort. From the American dream to corporate greed, Belfort goes from penny stocks and righteousness to IPOs and a life of corruption in the late 80s. Excess success and affluence in his early twenties as founder of the brokerage firm Stratton Oakmont warranted Belfort the title "The Wolf of Wall Street."

Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $171,499,882
